perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term confidential information (“Confidential Information”) shall mean all financial and other nonpublic information, together with notes, analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared or provided by the disclosing party in connection with the evaluation of the Acquisition.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term confidential information (“Confidential Information”) may include all financial and other nonpublic information, together with notes, analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared or provided by the disclosing party in connection with the evaluation of the Acquisition.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'shall mean' to 'may include' introduces ambiguity. The original definition is definitive, requiring all financial and nonpublic information to be considered Confidential Information. The modified version makes the inclusion of such information optional, creating uncertainty as to what exactly constitutes Confidential Information under the agreement. This contradicts the Non-Disclosure Clause, where it is not clear which information is protected, since "Confidential Information" is not clear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except to the extent permitted by paragraph 1.5 hereof, for a period of twelve (12) months following the conclusion of any discussions or negotiations relating to the Acquisition, the parties hereto will direct their respective Representatives to not disclose to any person or entity that the Confidential Information has been made available, that discussions or negotiations are taking place or have recently taken place concerning the Acquisition, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any other possible transaction between the parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall mean' to 'may include' introduces ambiguity into the definition of "Confidential Information." The original text provided a definitive definition, whereas the changed text makes the inclusion of financial and nonpublic information optional. This directly contradicts the Non-Disclosure Clause (Contradicted Text), because if the definition of Confidential Information is unclear, the scope of the Non-Disclosure Clause becomes unclear as well. This ambiguity regarding the scope of "Confidential Information" creates a clear contradiction, as per the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term representatives (“Representatives”) shall mean each party, their directors, officers and employees, as well as their counsel, accountants, consultants and other representatives in connection with the transactions contemplated hereby provided that such persons are bound by confidentiality agreements no less stringent than those in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term representatives (“Representatives”) shall mean each party, their directors, officers and employees, as well as their counsel, accountants, consultants and other representatives in connection with the transactions contemplated hereby whether or not such persons are bound by confidentiality agreements.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'provided that such persons are bound by confidentiality agreements no less stringent than those in this Agreement' introduces an internal contradiction. Initially, representatives were defined as those bound by confidentiality agreements. The modified definition includes representatives regardless of whether they're bound by such agreements. This contradicts the Use of Confidential Information section where 'Representatives' are allowed access to Confidential Information assuming they're bound by confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party will use the Confidential Information it receives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Acquisition and not for any other purpose and, except to the extent permitted by paragraph 1.5 hereof, will keep such Confidential Information strictly confidential, provided, however, that Confidential Information may be disclosed to such Representatives as needed to know such information for the purpose of evaluating and negotiating the terms of the Acquisition and for no other purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'Representatives' to include individuals not bound by confidentiality agreements. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which allows disclosure of Confidential Information only to Representatives 'as needed to know such information,' implying a condition of confidentiality. By removing the confidentiality requirement in the definition of 'Representatives,' the changed text creates a scenario where individuals without confidentiality obligations could access Confidential Information, directly conflicting with the intention and stipulation of the contradicted text. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction as it creates conflicting obligations regarding who is allowed access to confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Each party will use the Confidential Information it receives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Acquisition and not for any other purpose and, except to the extent permitted by paragraph 1.5 hereof, will keep such Confidential Information strictly confidential, provided, however, that Confidential Information may be disclosed to such Representatives as needed to know such information for the purpose of evaluating and negotiating the terms of the Acquisition and for no other purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party will use the Confidential Information it receives solely or partially for the purpose of evaluating the Acquisition and not primarily for any other purpose and, except to the extent permitted by paragraph 1.5 hereof, will keep such Confidential Information reasonably confidential, provided, however, that Confidential Information may be disclosed to such Representatives as needed to know such information.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes 'solely' to 'solely or partially' and inserts 'not primarily'. This allows for the Confidential Information to be used for purposes other than evaluating the Acquisition, and the level of confidentiality is reduced from 'strictly' to 'reasonably', adding to ambiguity. This creates a contradiction in the Non-Disclosure clause as there is no clear definition of "reasonably confidential", making it difficult to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except to the extent permitted by paragraph 1.5 hereof, for a period of twelve (12) months following the conclusion of any discussions or negotiations relating to the Acquisition, the parties hereto will direct their respective Representatives to not disclose to any person or entity that the Confidential Information has been made available, that discussions or negotiations are taking place or have recently taken place concerning the Acquisition, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any other possible transaction between the parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates that Confidential Information be used 'solely' for evaluating the Acquisition and kept 'strictly' confidential. The changed text modifies this to 'solely or partially' and 'reasonably confidential.' The contradicted text requires that parties not disclose that Confidential Information has been made available or any terms, conditions, or other facts with respect to any other possible transaction. By allowing the information to be used 'partially' for other purposes and only kept 'reasonably confidential,' the changed text creates a direct contradiction. It weakens the obligation of non-disclosure, potentially permitting actions that would violate the non-disclosure terms outlined in the contradicted text. The term 'reasonably confidential' is ambiguous and contradicts the implied strict confidentiality required to maintain the integrity of the discussions and negotiations described in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the parties hereto do not proceed with the Acquisition by October 31, 2007, and, in any event, within five (5) days after being so requested by either party, both parties shall return or destroy all documents furnished by the other.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the parties hereto do not proceed with the Acquisition by October 31, 2007, and, in any event, within five (5) days after being so requested by either party, both parties should consider returning or destroying some documents furnished by the other.
perturbation.explanation: The switch from 'shall return or destroy all documents' to 'should consider returning or destroying some documents' weakens the obligation to return or destroy confidential information. It introduces an option where previously there was a requirement. This change causes an internal contradiction, specifically with the confidentiality obligations outlined earlier, especially since some documents may not be destroyed, it continues being subject to confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party will use the Confidential Information it receives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Acquisition and not for any other purpose and, except to the extent permitted by paragraph 1.5 hereof, will keep such Confidential Information strictly confidential, provided, however, that Confidential Information may be disclosed to such Representatives as needed to know such information for the purpose of evaluating and negotiating the terms of the Acquisition and for no other purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the return or destruction of all documents, ensuring confidentiality. The changed text weakens this obligation by suggesting parties 'should consider' returning or destroying 'some' documents, introducing ambiguity and a loophole. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires parties to 'keep such Confidential Information strictly confidential'. The weakened obligation means some confidential documents may not be returned or destroyed, thus potentially compromising confidentiality and creating a direct contradiction as outlined in the explanation and adheres to the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Zulu and its principal shareholders agree that until October 31, 2007, or such shorter period if either party notifies the other that it no longer wishes to proceed with the Acquisition (“Term of this Agreement”), that they will not, directly or indirectly, solicit, initiate or encourage submission of proposals of offers from any third party relating to any acquisition, purchase or option to purchase an equity interest in Zulu, or any merger, consolidation or business combination with Zulu or the sale of substantially all of the assets of Zulu.
perturbation.changed_text: Zulu and its principal shareholders agree that until October 31, 2007, or such shorter period if either party notifies the other that it no longer wishes to proceed with the Acquisition (“Term of this Agreement”), that they will use their best efforts not to, directly or indirectly, solicit, initiate or encourage submission of proposals of offers from any third party relating to any acquisition, purchase or option to purchase an equity interest in Zulu, or any merger, consolidation or business combination with Zulu or the sale of substantially all of the assets of Zulu.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'use their best efforts' weakens the standstill provision. Originally, Zulu and its shareholders were strictly prohibited from soliciting offers. Now, they only have to try their best not to, which introduces ambiguity and makes the clause less enforceable. By stating that they will use their best effort, allows for other interpretations of the definition of that term, and this contradicts Injunction paragraph in ARTICLE 4, where a breach would be entitled to specific performance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties hereto agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that in addition to other remedies, each offended party shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief, and in such circumstances the offending party agrees to waive posting of a bond to secure any such equitable relief hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text created a strict prohibition against soliciting offers. The changed text weakens this prohibition by requiring only 'best efforts' not to solicit offers. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that any breach of the agreement entitles the offended party to specific performance and injunctive relief, because with the 'best efforts' clause, it is much harder to prove a breach, because best effort can be defined in many ways, making it uncertain whether specific performance is still an appropriate remedy, leading to a clear conflict.

perturbation.original_text: The parties hereto will use their best efforts to enter into a definitive acquisition agreement and any other documents that may be necessary in order to consummate the Acquisition by October 31, 2007. If a definitive agreement has not been entered into by such date, then this Agreement will have no further force or effect except for Articles 1, 3, 4, 5 and 6 hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties hereto will consider to use their best efforts to enter into a definitive acquisition agreement and any other documents that may be necessary in order to consummate the Acquisition by December 31, 2007. If a definitive agreement has not been entered into by such date, then this Agreement may have no further force or effect except for Articles 1, 3, 4, 5 and 6 hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes 'will use their best efforts' to 'will consider to use their best efforts', extends the deadline to December 31, 2007, and changes 'will have no further force or effect' to 'may have no further force or effect'. The introduction of 'consider' makes the obligation to use best efforts discretionary, the date is extended, and the effect of failing to meet the deadline is made uncertain. This weakens the obligation to enter into a definitive agreement by October 31 and creates an internal contradiction on when this agreement will be effective, by implying that the parties are not required to enter into the acquisition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Zulu and its principal shareholders agree that until October 31, 2007, or such shorter period if either party notifies the other that it no longer wishes to proceed with the Acquisition (“Term of this Agreement”), that they will not, directly or indirectly, solicit, initiate or encourage submission of proposals of offers from any third party relating to any acquisition, purchase or option to purchase an equity interest in Zulu, or any merger, consolidation or business combination with Zulu or the sale of substantially all of the assets of Zulu.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates that the parties 'will use their best efforts' to finalize the acquisition agreement by October 31, 2007. The changed text weakens this obligation by stating the parties 'will consider to use their best efforts' and extends the deadline to December 31, 2007. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that Zulu and its shareholders agree not to solicit other offers until October 31, 2007. The changed text, by extending the deadline and weakening the obligation, allows Zulu to solicit other offers after the original October 31 deadline, directly conflicting with the 'Contradicted Text'. Furthermore, the change from 'will have no further force or effect' to 'may have no further force or effect' introduces ambiguity regarding the agreement's validity after the deadline, adding another layer of contradiction.
